messages.content:
What is the full form of OARS?

a. Ownership, Age, race and summary
b. Openness, Aggravation, Reality & Summary
c. Open questions, Age, Reality & Summary
d. Open questions, Affirmations, Reflections and Summarize
d. Open questions, Affirmations, Reflections and Summarize

Which of the following technique should be used by the nurse to prevent the tracking of the medication if the IM injection prepared leads to irritation

1. Use small gauge needle
2. Use Z-track technique
3. Administer at 45 angel
4. Apply ice on injection site
2. Use Z-track technique

______ is a group of symptoms associated with a slow  progressive loss of intellectual functions — including attention, memory, judgement, language, orientation,  motor & spatial skills and is a disorder, not an identity

1. Dementia
2. Epilepsy
3. Retardation
4. Depersonalization
1. Dementia

You are the administrator of your community hospital. Your board of directors consists of a cross section of community leaders representing major businesses and organizations. It includes the elected representatives of the medical and professional staff. You are meeting with your board chairman to develop the agenda and various items for the next board meeting. An item for review, discussion, and approval is the new contract negotiated by your predecessor with the pathology group. One of the physician board members is president of the radiology group, with which preliminary contract discussions have begun. In addition, the board chairman indicates his concern that the hospital really has never developed any organized future planning. Managing equitable and fair relationships with hospital-based physicians is the responsibility of the administrator. In order to assure that proper consideration and decision-making will occur with regard to the pathology group, you should:


1. Exclude the radiologist board member from any contract discussions involving other physicians and/or groups.
2. Establish an organized well-defined format and methodology for contract negotiation and approval which is to be followed in all contractual relationships.
3. Secure board approval appropriately for all contract negotiations and agreements.
4. Alert the board at their next meeting that the physician members may be in a conflict-of-interest situation.
4. Alert the board at their next meeting that the physician members may be in a conflict-of-interest situation.

All of the below are important purpose for the documentation except:
To communicate
To assure the quality
To provide comfort
To reimbursement
To provide comfort

One patient is admitted in the hospital, which of the following event would lead the nurse to suspect the patient’s nutritional alterations:

1. Shiny hairs
2. Moist conjunctiva
3. Eye clear
4. Ridged nails
4. Ridged nails

which is a group of symptoms associated with a slow  progressive loss of intellectual functions — including attention, memory, judgement, language, orientation,  motor & spatial skills and is a disorder, not an identity ?

1. Dementia
2. Epilepsy
3. Retardation
4. Depersonalization
1. Dementia

Which of the following is appropriate for common ailment (Cough and fever) ?

1. Medicine at the drug store
2. Medicine in doctor’s clinic
3. Indigenous plants with proven benefits
4. Medicine at Botika
2. Medicine in doctor's clinic

The school nurse examines a 14-year-old male student, the student has fever from few days and usually has temperature of 102°F (38.9°C), a cough, and a generalized red, blotchy rash. What would the nurse do after notifying student’s parents and sending him back home ?:
Hold an immunization clinic.
Notify the parents of all students of a potential measles outbreak.
Report a suspected case of measles to the local health department
Review the records of all students to determine their immunization status
3. Report a suspected case of measles to the local health department

The school nurse examines a 14-year-old male student, the student has fever from few days and usually has temperature of 102°F (38.9°C), a cough, and a generalized red, blotchy rash. What would the nurse do after notifying student’s parents and sending him back home? :

1. Hold an immunization clinic.
2. Notify the parents of all students of a potential measles outbreak.
3. Report a suspected case of measles to the local health department
4. Review the records of all students to determine their immunization status
4. Review the records of all students to determine their immunization status

All of the below mentioned are the mistakes that is done by the head nurse during the time of performance appraisal except:

1. Paired Comparison
2. Halo effect
3. Central tendency error
4. Horns effect
1. Paired Comparison

which method is considered as the method of collaboration that is used for providing care to a group of patients under the direction of a professional nurse ?

1. Team
2. Case
3. Modular
4. Primary
4. Primary

Consider the following summary definitions of a small/basic health center. Which one would you choose as clear-cut criterion to classify as such a health facility in a poor, distressed health sector?

1. A health facility staffed by one community health worker and a traditional birth attendant, supplied by a ration kit of essential medicines.
2. A health facility staffed by one medical doctor, four nurses, two midwives and one lab technician, offering basic emergency care, inpatient care for acute medical conditions, and mother and child health care.
  3. A health facility staffed by one medical assistant, one or two nurses and one midwife, offering outpatient curative and mother and child health care (including immunizations).
4. A health facility of 100–150 square metres of covered surface, with a few beds, continuous electricity supply and running water
3. A health facility staffed by one medical assistant, one or two nurses and one midwife, offering outpatient curative and mother and child health care (including immunizations).

One patient is admitted in the hospital, which of the following event would lead the nurse to suspect the patient’s nutritional alterations?:

1. Shiny hairs
2. Moist conjunctiva
3. Eye clear
4. Ridged nails
4. Ridged nails
